---
# ── REQUIRED ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
draft: false                         # Set false when ready to publish
title: "Disaggregated Estimates of the Prevalence of Trafficking in Humans (DEPTH)"
description: "One-sentence summary shown in the listing grid and SEO."
date: 2026-01-01                     # Project start or publication date
date-modified: 2026-01-01           # Update when page is edited

# ── HOME PAGE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Set true to pin this project in the featured Projects section on the home page.
highlight: false

# ── TAXONOMY ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# First category drives the listing filter bar in projects.qmd.
# Active filter buttons: Supply Chains, Climate Modelling, Field Research
categories: [Category1, Category2]

# ── IMAGE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Card thumbnail in the projects listing grid.
# Options: img-network · img-scatter · img-timeseries · img-heatmap · img-sankey
image: ../assets/images/img-depth-logo.png

# ── STATUS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# active    → ongoing project, shows first in home page auto-selection
# in-review → under peer review
# published → completed and published
# archived  → no longer active
status: active

# ── PUBLICATION METADATA (if published) ────────────────────────────────────────
pub-journal: ""                      # Journal or venue name
doi: ""                              # DOI without https://doi.org/ prefix

# ── PROJECT WEBSITE / REPO (optional) ──────────────────────────────────────────
repo: https://nij.ojp.gov/funding/awards/15pnij-24-gg-01747-ht                            # URL to project website or code repository
---

[← Back to Projects](../projects.html){.back-link}

::: {.label}
Active · Funder · Year range
:::

<!-- ── OVERVIEW ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  2–3 paragraphs explaining the research question, why it matters, and the
  approach. Write for an informed non-specialist (funder, journalist, peer
  in an adjacent field). Avoid jargon without explanation.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

## Overview

Research question and context...

---

<!-- ── METHODS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Describe the methods in enough detail that a researcher could replicate or
  build on the work. Use ### sub-headings for multi-step workflows.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

## Methods

The project seeks to estimate the prevalence of human trafficking among cross border migrants along the Del Rio Corridor in Texas and in Tucson, Arizona. It uses a random sample of migrants, a co-designed survey instrument, and advanced statistical analysis, threshold protocols, and bootstrapping techniques. The prevalence estimation is complemented with secondary data analysis, key informant interviews, policy analysis, and systematic evidence reviews. 

### Data sources

...

### Analysis

...

---

<!-- ── KEY FINDINGS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Use a short numbered list for quantitative results; prose for interpretive
  findings. Avoid bullet points for anything that needs a sentence to explain.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

## Key findings

1. Finding one...
2. Finding two...

---

<!-- ── PUBLICATIONS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  List all outputs: papers, reports, datasets, visualisations.
  Use standard citation format with DOI links.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

## Publications

- Author, A. & Author, B. (Year). *Title.* Journal, vol(issue), pp. [doi:...](https://doi.org/...)

<!-- ── CALLOUT EXAMPLE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Use callout-note for methodological caveats, callout-tip for replication notes.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

::: {.callout-note}
In process
:::
